page: Vultr vx1-m-96c-768gb is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 96 shared AMD vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include dedicated GPU hardware.

Storage is provisioned as 1 GB of network-attached storage, indicating that primary storage requirements must be handled via external network volumes. The server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Because the 96 AMD vCPUs are allocated on a shared basis, this server offers a cost-effective path to acquiring a massive 768.0 GB memory footprint without the premium associated with dedicated CPU cores. However, the shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability under sustained, multi-core compute loads.

This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require continuous, guaranteed CPU performance. Appropriate use cases include large-scale caching layers, in-memory databases with bursty access patterns, and staging environments for memory-heavy applications.
description: Vultr vx1-m-96c-768gb is an x86_64 server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 96 shared AMD vCPUs and 768.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, making it a memory-optimized option. The instance does not include local storage, relying instead on 1 GB of network storage, and contains no GPU accelerators. Because the CPU allocation is shared, the server offers high memory density at a lower cost profile, though it introduces potential CPU performance variability under sustained loads. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads with bursty CPU demands, such as large-memory caching, staging environments, and non-production database deployments.

tagline: A high-density memory configuration featuring shared AMD processors and network storage for memory-intensive, bursty workloads.

768.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
